SEARCH
Package com.shanebeestudios.vf.api

Class FurnaceManager

java.lang.Object
com.shanebeestudios.vf.api.FurnaceManager
public class FurnaceManager extends Object
Manager for Machines

You can get an instance of this class from VirtualFurnaceAPI.getFurnaceManager()

  • Method Details

    • getAllFurnaces

      @Deprecated(since="1.1.0") public Collection<Furnace> getAllFurnaces()
      Deprecated.
      Use getAllMachines() instead
      Get a collection of all Furnaces
      Returns:
      Collection of all furnaces

    • getAllMachines

      public Collection<Machine> getAllMachines()
      Get a collection of all Machines
      Returns:
      Collection of all machines

    • getByID

      public Machine getByID(@NotNull @NotNull UUID uuid)
      Get a Machine by ID
      Parameters:
      uuid - ID of machine to grab
      Returns:
      Machine from ID (null if a machine with this ID does not exist)

    • createFurnace

      public Furnace createFurnace(@NotNull @NotNull String name)
      Create a new furnace

      This will create a new furnace, add it to the tick list, and save to file

      NOTE: The default FurnaceProperties associated with this furnace will be FurnaceProperties.FURNACE

      Parameters:
      name - Name of new furnace (This shows up in the inventory view)
      Returns:
      Instance of this new furnace

    • createFurnace

      public Furnace createFurnace(@NotNull @NotNull String name, @NotNull @NotNull FurnaceProperties furnaceProperties)
      Create a new furnace

      This will create a new furnace, add it to the tick list, and save to file

      Parameters:
      name - Name of new furnace (This shows up in the inventory view)
      furnaceProperties - Properties to apply to this furnace
      Returns:
      Instance of this new furnace

    • createFurnace

      public Furnace createFurnace(@NotNull @NotNull String name, @NotNull @NotNull Consumer<Furnace> function)
      Create a new furnace

      This will create a new furnace, add it to the tick list, and save to file

      NOTE: The default FurnaceProperties associated with this furnace will be FurnaceProperties.FURNACE

      Parameters:
      name - Name of new furnace (This shows up in the inventory view)
      function - Function to run before furnace is created
      Returns:
      Instance of this new furnace

    • createFurnace

      public Furnace createFurnace(@NotNull @NotNull String name, @NotNull @NotNull FurnaceProperties furnaceProperties, @Nullable @Nullable Consumer<Furnace> function)
      Create a new furnace

      This will create a new furnace, add it to the tick list, and save to file

      Parameters:
      name - Name of new furnace (This shows up in the inventory view)
      furnaceProperties - Properties to apply to this furnace
      function - Function to run before furnace is created
      Returns:
      Instance of this new furnace

    • createItemWithFurnace

      public ItemStack createItemWithFurnace(@NotNull @NotNull String name, @NotNull @NotNull Material material, boolean glowing)
      Create a Furnace that is attached to an ItemStack

      NOTE: The default FurnaceProperties associated with this furnace will be FurnaceProperties.FURNACE

      Parameters:
      name - Name of furnace (this will show up in the furnace UI)
      material - Material of the new ItemStack
      glowing - Whether the item should glow (enchanted)
      Returns:
      New ItemStack with a furnace attached

    • createItemWithFurnace

      public ItemStack createItemWithFurnace(@NotNull @NotNull String name, @NotNull @NotNull FurnaceProperties furnaceProperties, @NotNull @NotNull Material material, boolean glowing)
      Create a Furnace that is attached to an ItemStack
      Parameters:
      name - Name of furnace (this will show up in the furnace UI)
      furnaceProperties - Properties associated with this furnace item
      material - Material of the new ItemStack
      glowing - Whether the item should glow (enchanted)
      Returns:
      New ItemStack with a furnace attached

    • createItemWithFurnace

      public ItemStack createItemWithFurnace(@NotNull @NotNull String name, @NotNull @NotNull Material material, boolean glowing, @Nullable @Nullable Consumer<Furnace> function)
      Create a Furnace that is attached to an ItemStack

      NOTE: The default FurnaceProperties associated with this furnace will be FurnaceProperties.FURNACE

      Parameters:
      name - Name of furnace (this will show up in the furnace UI)
      material - Material of the new ItemStack
      glowing - Whether the item should glow (enchanted)
      function - Function to run before furnace is created
      Returns:
      New ItemStack with a furnace attached

    • createItemWithFurnace

      public ItemStack createItemWithFurnace(@NotNull @NotNull String name, @NotNull @NotNull FurnaceProperties furnaceProperties, @NotNull @NotNull Material material, boolean glowing, @Nullable @Nullable Consumer<Furnace> function)
      Create a Furnace that is attached to an ItemStack
      Parameters:
      name - Name of furnace (this will show up in the furnace UI)
      furnaceProperties - Properties associated with this furnace item
      material - Material of the new ItemStack
      glowing - Whether the item should glow (enchanted)
      function - Function to run before furnace is created
      Returns:
      New ItemStack with a furnace attached

    • createItemWithFurnace

      public ItemStack createItemWithFurnace(@NotNull @NotNull String name, @NotNull @NotNull ItemStack itemStack, boolean glowing)
      Create a Furnace that is attached to an ItemStack
      Parameters:
      name - Name of furnace (this will show up in the furnace UI)
      itemStack - ItemStack to be copied and have a furnace attached
      glowing - Whether the item should glow (enchanted)
      Returns:
      Clone of the input ItemStack with a furnace attached

    • createItemWithFurnace

      public ItemStack createItemWithFurnace(@NotNull @NotNull String name, @NotNull @NotNull FurnaceProperties furnaceProperties, @NotNull @NotNull ItemStack itemStack, boolean glowing)
      Create a Furnace that is attached to an ItemStack
      Parameters:
      name - Name of furnace (this will show up in the furnace UI)
      furnaceProperties - Properties associated with this furnace item
      itemStack - ItemStack to be copied and have a furnace attached
      glowing - Whether the item should glow (enchanted)
      Returns:
      Clone of the input ItemStack with a furnace attached

    • createItemWithFurnace

      public ItemStack createItemWithFurnace(@NotNull @NotNull String name, @NotNull @NotNull ItemStack itemStack, boolean glowing, @Nullable @Nullable Consumer<Furnace> function)
      Create a Furnace that is attached to an ItemStack

      NOTE: The default FurnaceProperties associated with this furnace will be FurnaceProperties.FURNACE

      Parameters:
      name - Name of furnace (this will show up in the furnace UI)
      itemStack - ItemStack to be copied and have a furnace attached
      glowing - Whether the item should glow (enchanted)
      function - Function to run before furnace is created
      Returns:
      Clone of the input ItemStack with a furnace attached

    • createItemWithFurnace

      public ItemStack createItemWithFurnace(@NotNull @NotNull String name, @NotNull @NotNull FurnaceProperties furnaceProperties, @NotNull @NotNull ItemStack itemStack, boolean glowing, @Nullable @Nullable Consumer<Furnace> function)
      Create a Furnace that is attached to an ItemStack
      Parameters:
      name - Name of furnace (this will show up in the furnace UI)
      furnaceProperties - Properties associated with this furnace item
      itemStack - ItemStack to be copied and have a furnace attached
      glowing - Whether the item should glow (enchanted)
      function - Function to run before furnace is created
      Returns:
      Clone of the input ItemStack with a furnace attached

    • getFurnaceFromItemStack

      @Deprecated(since="1.1.0") public Furnace getFurnaceFromItemStack(@NotNull @NotNull ItemStack itemStack)
      Deprecated.
      Use getMachineFromItemStack(ItemStack) instead
      Get a Furnace from an ItemStack
      Parameters:
      itemStack - ItemStack to grab furnace from
      Returns:
      Furnace if the ItemStack has one assigned to it else null

    • getMachineFromItemStack

      public Machine getMachineFromItemStack(@NotNull @NotNull ItemStack itemStack)
      Get a Machine from an ItemStack
      Parameters:
      itemStack - ItemStack to grab machine from
      Returns:
      Machine if the ItemStack has one assigned to it else null

    • saveFurnace

      public void saveFurnace(@NotNull @NotNull Machine machine, boolean saveToFile)
      Save a machine to YAML storage

      NOTE: If choosing not to save to file, this change will not take effect in the YAML file, this may be useful for saving a large batch and saving file at the end of the batch change, use saveConfig() to save all changes to file

      Parameters:
      machine - Machine to save
      saveToFile - Whether to save to file

    • removeFurnaceFromConfig

      public void removeFurnaceFromConfig(@NotNull @NotNull Machine machine, boolean saveToFile)
      Remove a machine from YAML storage

      NOTE: If choosing not to save to file, this change will not take effect in the YAML file, this may be useful if removing a large batch and saving file at the end of the batch change, use saveConfig() to save all changes to file

      Parameters:
      machine - Machine to remove
      saveToFile - Whether to save changes to file

    • removeMachine

      public void removeMachine(@NotNull @NotNull Machine machine, boolean saveToFile)
      Remove a Machine

      This will remove from memory and from file (if saving is true)

      Parameters:
      machine - Machine to remove
      saveToFile - Whether to save to file

    • saveAll

      public void saveAll()
      Save all furnaces to file

    • saveConfig

      public void saveConfig()
      Save current furnace YAML from RAM to file
